skill.name: free-tool-strategy
skill.description: Plan, evaluate, and scope free marketing tools that support lead generation, SEO traffic, and brand awareness.
skill.summary: Create practical free tool strategies for growth, leads, and organic traffic.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks, weak cryptographic terms, and reconnaissance terms. Manual review found these are Markdown templates and ordinary marketing language, with no executable code, network calls, filesystem access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown code fences around ROI and output-format examples. They do not instruct the assistant to execute shell commands or run code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks delimit static Markdown examples. No command text, shell invocation, interpreter call, or user-controlled execution path appears in these lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Terms
low_findings.description: The reported weak-crypto hits are ordinary words in strategy guidance, such as lead, before, design, and description. They are not cryptographic algorithm references or security implementation guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is marketing planning text. No hashing, encryption, key handling, or cryptographic API usage is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Term
low_findings.description: The reported system reconnaissance hit is UTM attribution advice for paid promotion. It does not gather host, operating system, process, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line refers to marketing campaign tracking parameters. There is no command, API call, or instruction to inspect a user's system.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The reported network reconnaissance hits are references to desired market data and sales or product links. They do not scan networks, query endpoints, or enumerate infrastructure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines are business strategy guidance. No network tools, URLs, requests, sockets, or scanning behavior are described.

content.value_statement: Many teams want free tools for growth but struggle to choose ideas with clear business value. This skill guides strategy, scoping, lead capture, SEO planning, and measurement.
actual_capabilities: Guides discovery questions for audience, goals, resources, and business fit.
Compares calculators, generators, analyzers, validators, and resource libraries.
Scores tool ideas against search demand, buyer match, build feasibility, and maintenance burden.
Outlines lead capture approaches, including gated, partially gated, ungated, and optional capture models.
Produces a structured strategy document with concept, SEO, build scope, metrics, promotion, and timeline.
limitations: Does not build the tool implementation by itself.
Does not provide live keyword volume, competitor data, or traffic estimates without external research.
Does not replace legal, privacy, or compliance review for lead capture forms.
Does not validate technical feasibility beyond planning-level guidance.

use_cases.title: Prioritize a SaaS Growth Tool
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether an ROI calculator, readiness quiz, or template library can attract qualified product leads.

use_cases.title: Scope an Agency Lead Magnet
use_cases.description: Turn a service buyer pain point into a calculator, audit, or benchmark tool with clear capture and follow-up steps.

use_cases.title: Plan an SEO Resource
use_cases.description: Choose a free resource format that can rank for tool-related searches and support ongoing content promotion.

prompt_templates.title: Brainstorm Free Tool Ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me brainstorm free tool ideas for my business. My product is [product], my audience is [audience], and my main goal is [leads, SEO, or awareness].

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Tool Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this free tool idea: [idea]. Score it for search demand, buyer fit, uniqueness, product connection, build feasibility, maintenance, links, and share value.

prompt_templates.title: Create MVP Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an MVP scope for [tool idea]. Include core inputs, outputs, lead capture, technical approach, must-have features, skipped features, and success metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full free tool strategy for [business]. Include audience, concept, SEO opportunity, lead capture, build approach, promotion plan, metrics, timeline, and risks.

output_examples.input: A B2B SaaS company wants a free tool to generate qualified leads.
output_examples.output: The skill may recommend a product ROI calculator, explain why it matches buyer intent, define a partial-gate capture flow, and propose metrics such as completions, emails captured, and influenced pipeline.

output_examples.input: An agency wants to test whether a project scoping calculator is worth building.
output_examples.output: The skill may score the idea across buyer match, build effort, SEO value, and maintenance, then suggest an MVP with simple inputs, a scoped estimate, and a follow-up consultation offer.

output_examples.input: A content team needs a free resource for organic growth.
output_examples.output: The skill may compare a template library, checklist generator, and analyzer, then recommend the option with the clearest keyword strategy and strongest product connection.

best_practices: Start with a specific audience problem before choosing a tool format.
Keep the first version small, useful, mobile-friendly, and easy to complete.
Connect the tool output to a natural next step in the product or sales journey.
anti_patterns: Building a tool only because competitors have one.
Gating every interaction before the user receives value.
Choosing a complex idea without maintenance capacity or clear attribution.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps plan and evaluate free marketing tools that can generate leads, attract search traffic, or build awareness.

faq.question: Can it build the tool?
faq.answer: It can create a strategy and MVP scope, but it does not implement the software by itself.

faq.question: What tool types does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers calculators, generators, analyzers, auditors, validators, libraries, and resource tools.

faq.question: Does it require keyword research data?
faq.answer: No, but real search volume and competitor research will improve the strategy.

faq.question: How does it handle lead capture?
faq.answer: It compares gated, partially gated, ungated, and optional capture models based on value and friction.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Founders, growth marketers, agencies, content teams, and technical marketers can use it to choose and scope free tools.
